content.title: Uday Varmora - Cloud / DevOps Engineer Resume
content.summary: Cloud / DevOps Engineer with **1+ year** designing **CI/CD pipelines and cloud infrastructure** for **scalable, secure, and high-availability systems** — cutting release cycles **82% (45 → 8 min)**, provisioning **80%**, and toil **70%** across 20+ services. Hands-on with **Jenkins, GitLab CI/CD, GitHub Actions, AWS CodePipeline, Docker, Kubernetes (EKS), Terraform, Python/Bash**, with proven **environment management across multiple projects** and focus on **cost efficiency and modular, reusable code**.
skills: Jenkins, GitLab CI/CD, GitHub Actions, AWS CodePipeline, AWS CodeBuild, ArgoCD, Release Management
Terraform (34 reusable modules), AWS CloudFormation, AWS SAM, ARM templates, Ansible
AWS — EKS, EC2, Lambda, S3, ECR, RDS, VPC, CloudWatch; Azure (Fundamentals)
Docker, Kubernetes (EKS), Helm, Karpenter, KEDA, Ingress-NGINX
Python, Bash/Shell, Linux (Ubuntu, Amazon Linux, RHEL), System Administration
Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, ELK Stack, CloudWatch, Datadog
HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, Sealed Secrets, Trivy Operator, IAM, KMS, TLS/SSL
Git, GitHub, Bitbucket, Branching Strategies, Code Review Workflows

bullets: Designed and maintained **40+ CI/CD pipelines** using **Jenkins, GitLab CI/CD, GitHub Actions, AWS CodePipeline, and AWS CodeBuild** — automating build-test-deploy workflows for **scalable applications and data platforms** across **20+ Dockerised microservices**, cutting release cycles by **82% (45 min → under 8 min)** with zero manual intervention.

Provisioned **multi-project environment management** on **Linux (Ubuntu, Amazon Linux, RHEL)** using **Terraform IaC** (34 reusable modules, S3 remote state, DynamoDB locking) — reducing provisioning time by **80%** and configuration drift by **95%** across Dev, Staging, and Production environments.

Operated **Kubernetes (EKS)** clusters with **ArgoCD GitOps, Helm, and Ingress-NGINX Controller** — managing zero-downtime rollouts, Karpenter node autoscaling, and KEDA event-driven scaling; sustained **99.9% uptime** across multi-AZ clusters supporting advanced analytics solutions.

Eliminated **~70% of recurring operational toil** by writing **modular, reusable, and functional Python and Bash code** for cluster workflows, log triage, secret rotation, and pipeline scripting; integrated SonarQube quality gates to enforce strict security **compliance**.

Optimized **cloud resource utilization for performance and cost efficiency** — reduced compute costs by **30% ($500+/month)** through Karpenter Spot/On-Demand diversification and KEDA off-hours scale-down, preserving production availability across 20+ services.

Built and maintained AWS infrastructure monitoring on **Linux-based environments**, configured CloudWatch dashboards, and triaged production incidents across Kubernetes workloads; authored operational runbooks for recurring issue patterns and root-cause analyses.

Wrote **Python and Bash** automation scripts to streamline deployment workflows and reduce manual operational steps — demonstrated ownership and impact, earning **full-time promotion within 5 months**.

projects.title: Cloud-Native SaaS CRM Platform — Multi-Tenant AWS / EKS Build & Deploy Infrastructure
projects.tech: Terraform · AWS EKS · Docker · Jenkins · GitLab CI/CD · GitHub Actions · AWS CodePipeline · ArgoCD · Ingress-NGINX · Prometheus · Grafana
bullets: Designed multi-environment build/deploy architecture with per-environment **VPC isolation and S3/DynamoDB remote state** — enabling reproducible Dev/Production infrastructure on Linux-based EKS nodes with **Ingress-NGINX Controller**; executed zero-downtime **EKS cluster upgrade (v1.33 → v1.34)** managing node group rollouts, add-on compatibility, and API deprecation remediation.

Reduced compute costs by **30% ($500+/month)** via Karpenter Spot/On-Demand diversification and KEDA off-hours scale-down; migrated deprecated loki-stack to Loki + Grafana Alloy, eliminating deprecated chart dependencies and improving log pipeline reliability.

Built multi-region **disaster recovery strategy** using Velero (with EBS snapshot Data Mover), S3/ECR cross-region replication, and AWS Backup with cross-region vaults — achieving **RPO < 1 hour** for production workloads.

projects.title: Serverless Backend — Multi-Tenant Salon Management SaaS
projects.tech: AWS Lambda · SAM · CloudFormation · API Gateway · RDS PostgreSQL · Amazon EFS · Secrets Manager · Python
bullets: Architected a serverless build/deploy system using **6 nested SAM/CloudFormation templates** with SAM build pipelines — enabling independent deployment of **30+ Python Lambda functions** with reproducible packaging and per-function least-privilege IAM.
Solved Lambda layer size constraints by engineering a shared **EFS-mounted Python dependency layer** across 30+ functions — eliminating packaging bottlenecks and cutting build times by **60%+**.

projects.title: Healthcare Management System — Cloud Infrastructure & CI/CD
projects.tech: AWS S3 · CloudFront · App Runner · ECR · Docker · GitHub Actions · Secrets Manager · IAM
bullets: Engineered split-hosting (S3/CloudFront frontend, Dockerised App Runner backend) with a **dual-registry GHCR/ECR strategy** — enabling zero-downtime redeployments and **~30% reduced image pull latency** while serving 500+ concurrent users.

Automated full release workflow via **GitHub Actions** — Docker build, ECR/GHCR dual-push, S3 sync, CloudFront invalidation, and App Runner deploy; applied least-privilege IAM scoping that removed 5+ overly permissive roles.

education.left: **B.Tech, Information Technology** — Dharmsinh Desai University, Gujarat, India  |  CGPA: 7.25 / 10

achievements: Awarded **“Student of the Year”** in Class 10 for outstanding academic performance and leadership contributions.

jd:
Job highlights
Bachelor's in Computer Science or related field with 2-5 years in DevOps, cloud engineering, expertise in AWS/Azure, CI/CD tools, Docker, Kubernetes, and IaC
Design and maintain CI/CD pipelines, manage cloud infrastructure, implement IaC, containerize applications, monitor systems, troubleshoot issues, and ensure security and cost efficiency
Job match score
Early Applicant
Keyskills
Location
Work Experience
Job description

About you

Bachelors degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related technical field.
25 years of hands-on experience in DevOps, cloud engineering, or platform engineering roles.
Strong experience with cloud platforms such as AWS and/or Azure.
Proficiency in CI/CD tools (e.g., GitHub Actions, Jenkins, Azure DevOps) for automated build, test, and deployment pipelines.
Hands-on experience with containerization and orchestration tools such as Docker and Kubernetes.
Strong scripting/programming skills in Python, Bash, or similar languages.
Experience with Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools such as Terraform, ARM templates, or CloudFormation.
Familiarity with monitoring and logging tools (e.g., Prometheus, Grafana, ELK stack, CloudWatch).
Understanding of networking fundamentals, system architecture, and security best practices.
Experience with version control systems (Git) and collaborative development workflows.

Who you will work with

The Consumer Products Center of Expertise collaborates with Bains global Consumer Products Practice leadership, client-facing Bain leadership and teams, and with end clients on development and delivery of Bain’s proprietary CP products and solutions. These solutions aim to answer strategic questions of Bain’s CP clients relating to brand strategy (consumer needs, assortment, pricing, distribution), revenue growth management (pricing strategy, promotions, profit pools, trade terms), negotiation strategy with key retailers, optimization of COGS etc.

You will work as part of the team in CP CoE comprising of a mix of Director, Managers, Projects Leads, Associates and Analysts working to implement cloud-based end-to-end advanced analytics solutions. Delivery models on projects vary from working as part of a CP Center of Expertise, broader global Bain case team within the CP ringfence, or within other industry CoEs such as FS / Retail / TMT / Energy / CME / etc with BCN on need basis.



What you’ll do

Design, implement, and maintain CI/CD pipelines to enable reliable and automated deployment of applications and data platforms.
Build and manage cloud infrastructure (Azure/ AWS) to support scalable, secure, and high-availability systems.
Implement Infrastructure as Code (IaC) to provision and manage infrastructure in a consistent and repeatable manner.
Containerize applications and manage deployments using Docker and orchestration platforms such as Kubernetes.
Set up and maintain monitoring, logging, and alerting systems to ensure system reliability and uptime.
Troubleshoot infrastructure, deployment, and performance issues across environments (dev, staging, production).
Collaborate with engineering and data teams to streamline development workflows and improve system performance.
Ensure adherence to security, compliance, and best practices across infrastructure and deployment pipelines.
Optimize cloud resource utilization for performance and cost efficiency.
Support release management and environment management across multiple projects and teams.

What you’ll do

The AS is expected to have a knack for seeking out challenging problems and producing their own ideas, which they will be encouraged to brainstorm with their peers and managers. They should be willing to learn new techniques and be open to solving problems with an interdisciplinary approach. They must have excellent coding skills and should demonstrate a willingness to write modular, reusable, and functional code.


Role: Associate / Consultant
Industry Type: Management Consulting
Department: Consulting
Employment Type: Full Time, Permanent
